prompt:
Help frame the research described in the benchmark case as a concrete business-research problem. Preserve the supplied model and identify claims or information that remain unsupported.

Condition instructions:
Respond as you normally would to this user request using only the serialized case material. Do not assume facts that are not supplied.

Benchmark case:
```json
{
  "adversarial": false,
  "decision_time_facts": [
    "risk scores",
    "expected-harm estimates",
    "review capacity"
  ],
  "domain": "IS",
  "evidence_needs": [
    {
      "claim": "Scores calibrate violation risk",
      "source_needed": false,
      "status": "model_assumption"
    }
  ],
  "fidelity_requirements": [
    "True status is unknown at selection",
    "Review capacity binds"
  ],
  "hard_failure_traps": [
    "future_or_hidden_information",
    "state_as_decision"
  ],
  "id": "is-05",
  "model_specification": {
    "constraints": [
      "at most 200 reviews"
    ],
    "decision_variables": [
      "flagged items selected for review"
    ],
    "mathematical_objective": "maximize expected prevented harm from reviewed items",
    "mechanism": "Selecting an item reveals its status and enables removal if violating; unselected items receive no intervention.",
    "model_type": "capacitated review selection",
    "state_variables": [
      "true violation status",
      "review finding"
    ],
    "uncertainty": "true violation status"
  },
  "prohibited_inferences": [
    "The manager knows which flags are violations"
  ],
  "prompt": "A trust-and-safety team can manually inspect only 200 flagged items during a shift. Before true violations are learned, the manager selects which flags to review using risk scores and expected harm. Unreviewed flags remain online. Frame scarce review attention without assuming perfect risk ranking.",
  "tags": [
    "resource-constrained-review",
    "moderation",
    "capacity"
  ],
  "title": "Resource-constrained review queue prioritization"
}
```
